title: README relative links break on PyPI (long_description)
evidence: README.md lines 1,11,34,35,386,390,393 use relative links (docs/API.md, docs/CLI.md, FUNCTIONAL_SPEC.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE, NOTICE). PyPI renders README as long_description but does NOT resolve relative repo paths.
impact: PyPI users see broken links (the v1.1.3 problem the user reported); cannot reach API/CLI/SPEC/CHANGELOG/LICENSE from the package page.
recommended_action: Convert README relative cross-doc/license links to absolute GitHub URLs pinned to the release version tag.

title: v1.2.0 git tag is stale (predates 9 commits on main incl. bidi + hero image); tag != shipped content
evidence: PyPI latest published = 1.1.3. A v1.2.0 git tag + GitHub release exist but were NEVER uploaded to PyPI. Since 1.1.3 the code gained additive public features (colspan, set_color/--no-color, set_bidi/--no-bidi, showcase); backward compatible => minor bump 1.1.3->1.2.0, which pyproject/__version__ ALREADY state. No version bump needed. BUT the v1.2.0 tag points at a commit 9 behind HEAD and does not contain the bidi feature or vistab-show-showcase-01.png, so /v1.2.0/ URLs to the hero image would 404.
impact: Version number is correct (1.2.0). Risk is only that the tag is stale vs shipped content
recommended_action: so version-pinned URLs would resolve to pre-bidi docs and 404 on the hero image.
public_behavior_change: Keep version 1.2.0. At release, MOVE the v1.2.0 tag to the release HEAD (safe: 1.2.0 was never on PyPI) so /v1.2.0/ URLs match the shipped package. Record as a release-checklist step, not a code change.
column17: Release-checklist item in S8; correct earlier mis-finding

title: Correction: no version bump to 1.3.0 (earlier finding was measured from tag not PyPI)
evidence: Initial finding proposed 1.3.0 by measuring from the git tag. Corrected: semver is measured from the last PUBLISHED version (1.1.3). Cumulative changes since 1.1.3 are additive => 1.2.0, already set. This supersedes the 1.3.0 proposal.

title: Version-pinned asset URLs would 404 for hero image on old tag
evidence: README image URLs use /main/. Hero vistab-show-showcase-01.png is not in v1.2.0 tag. Pinning to /v1.2.0/ would 404. Correct pin target is the NEXT release tag (v1.3.0), which will contain all current assets.
impact: If links are pinned to a tag that predates an asset
public_behavior_change: Pin README image + doc links to the version being released (v1.3.0), and make 'create+push tag before/at release' an explicit release-checklist step so the pinned URLs resolve.

title: Cross-doc nav bars in docs/*.md are GitHub-relative (fine on GitHub; not shipped to PyPI)
evidence: docs/CLI.md, docs/API.md, FUNCTIONAL_SPEC.md, CHANGELOG.md nav bars use relative links. Only README.md is long_description, so these are not PyPI-broken. They work on GitHub.
impact: Low: consistency only; no PyPI breakage.
recommended_action: Leave relative (correct for GitHub in-repo nav) OR optionally align; not a release blocker.

title: pyproject description stale/generic vs actual capabilities
evidence: pyproject description = 'A simple module for creating ASCII tables' understates (colspan, themes, RTL bidi, CJK, wrapping). PyPI summary line.
recommended_action: Update description to reflect current capabilities.

title: No Project-URLs in pyproject (Homepage/Docs/Source/Changelog)
evidence: pyproject has no [project.urls]. PyPI sidebar links (Homepage, Source, Documentation, Changelog) are absent.
impact: Users on PyPI have no direct links to repo/docs/changelog.

title: CHANGELOG [Unreleased] mis-attributes shipped 1.2.0 content
evidence: bidi/showcase/colspan-maxwidth/CLI.md-link were under [Unreleased] but ship AS 1.2.0 (first 1.2.0 to hit PyPI). Also the 1.2.0 section had duplicate Added/Changed/Fixed subsections.
impact: PyPI/GitHub changelog would omit headline features from the version that introduces them.
recommended_action: Fold [Unreleased] into [1.2.0]; consolidate duplicate subsections into one Added/Changed/Fixed.

title: 8 executed/-located plans carried stale pending/proposed Status lines; prompts dir not inventoried in first pass
evidence: Section-1 discovery in the first pass inventoried .agents/plans/pending/ (empty) but did NOT enumerate .agents/prompts/ nor check for status/location mismatches. User-surfaced .agents/prompts/executed/20260710-has-header-false-alignment-bug.md prompted the re-check. Found 8 files in .agents/plans/executed/ whose Status said PENDING/PROPOSED/not-executed while physically in executed/. Verified all their features are shipped/tested/green (has_header fix at src/vistab.py:3053 + TestHasHeaderAlignment; colspan; CLI verbs; library-first; --no-color). Stale metadata, not hidden work.
impact: Low: not a release blocker (work is shipped)
recommended_action: but dishonest lifecycle metadata that a future review/reader would distrust and that could mask a genuinely pending item.
public_behavior_change: Corrected all 8 Status lines to EXECUTED with a dated correction note; verified prompts/plans pending dirs are empty. Process fix: future Section-1 must enumerate .agents/prompts/ and check status/location consistency.
next_step: grep shows no stale pending statuses; pending dirs empty
